volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Ion (Section 1261 - Section 1346)

sections.label: Section 1261
sections.original: ὦ ταυρόμορφον ὄμμα Κηφισοῦ πατρός ,
sections.translation: O father Cephissus , with the bull-shaped head , what a viper is this thy child , or dragon with fiery eyes that dart a murderous gleam , in whose heart is throned incarnate daring ,

sections.label: Section 1265
sections.original: Γοργοῦς σταλαγμῶν , οἷς ἔμελλέ με κτενεῖν .
sections.translation: noxious as those Gorgon drops of venom wherewith she sought to compass my death . Seize her , that the peaks of Parnassus may card the flowing tresses of her hair , for thence shall she be hurled headlong amid the rocks . My lucky star hath kept me

sections.label: Section 1270
sections.original: μολεῖν Ἀθηνῶν χὑπὸ μητρυιὰν πεσεῖν .
sections.translation: from going to Athens , there to fall beneath the power of a step-mother . For I have gauged thy feelings towards me — the full extent of thy bitter hostility — whilst yet amongst my friends ; for hadst thou once shut me tip within thy house , my road to Hades ’ halls had led direct from thence .

sections.label: Section 1275
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὔτε βωμὸς οὔτʼ Ἀπόλλωνος δόμος
sections.translation: This altar shall not save thee , nor yet Apollo ’ s courts , for that pity thou implorest cries out more loudly for me and my mother , who , though absent in the flesh , is never in name far from me .

sections.label: Section 1280
sections.original: οἵαν ἔπλεξε · βωμὸν ἔπτηξεν θεοῦ ,
sections.translation: Behold this cursed woman , see the web of trickery she hath woven ! yet comes she cowering to Apollo ’ s altar , thinking to escape the punishment of her misdeeds .

sections.label: Section 1282
sections.original: ἀπεννέπω σε μὴ κατακτείνειν ἐμὲ
sections.translation: I warn thee not to slay me , both in my own name and in his at whose altar I am stationed .

sections.label: Section 1284
sections.original: τί δʼ ἐστὶ Φοίβῳ σοί τε κοινὸν ἐν μέσῳ ;
sections.translation: What hast thou to do with Phoebus ?

sections.label: Section 1285
sections.original: ἱερὸν τὸ σῶμα τῷ θεῷ δίδωμʼ ἔχειν .
sections.translation: This body I devote unto that god to keep .

sections.label: Section 1286
sections.original: κἄπειτʼ ἔκαινες φαρμάκοις τὸν τοῦ θεοῦ ;
sections.translation: And yet thou wert for poisoning his minister ?

sections.label: Section 1287
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐκέτʼ ἦσθα Λοξίου , πατρὸς δὲ σοῦ .
sections.translation: But thou wert not Apollo ’ s any longer , but thy father ’ s .

sections.label: Section 1288
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐγενόμεσθα πατρός · οὐσίαν λέγω .
sections.translation: Nay , I was his son , that is , in absence of a real father .

sections.label: Section 1289
sections.original: οὐκοῦν τότʼ ἦσθα · νῦν δʼ ἐγώ , σὺ δʼ οὐκέτι .
sections.translation: Thou wert so then ; now ’ tis I , not thou , who am Apollo ’ s .

sections.label: Section 1290
sections.original: οὐκ εὐσεβεῖς γε · τἀμὰ δʼ εὐσεβῆ τότʼ ἦν .
sections.translation: Well , thou art not guiltless now , whereas I was then .

sections.label: Section 1291
sections.original: ἔκτεινά σʼ ὄντα πολέμιον δόμοις ἐμοῖς .
sections.translation: I sought to slay thee as an enemy to my house .

sections.label: Section 1292
sections.original: οὔτοι σὺν ὅπλοις ἦλθον ἐς τὴν σὴν χθόνα .
sections.translation: And yet I never invaded thy country , sword in hand .

sections.label: Section 1293
sections.original: μάλιστα · κἀπίμπρης γʼ Ἐρεχθέως δόμους .
sections.translation: Thou didst ; and thou it was that wert casting a firebrand into the halls of Erechtheus .

sections.label: Section 1294
sections.original: ποίοισι πανοῖς ἢ πυρὸς ποίᾳ φλογί ;
sections.translation: What sort of brand or flaming fire was it ?

sections.label: Section 1295
sections.original: ἔμελλες οἰκεῖν τἄμʼ , ἐμοῦ βίᾳ λαβών .
sections.translation: Thou didst design to seize my home against my will , and make it thine .

sections.label: Section 1296
sections.original: πατρός γε γῆν διδόντος ἣν ἐκτήσατο .
sections.translation: What ! when my father offered me a kingdom of his getting .

sections.label: Section 1297
sections.original: τοῖς Αἰόλου δὲ πῶς μετῆν τῶν Παλλάδος ;
sections.translation: How had the sons of Aeolus any share in the realm of Pallas ?

sections.label: Section 1298
sections.original: ὅπλοισιν αὐτήν , οὐ λόγοις ἐρρύσατο .
sections.translation: Arms , not words , he brought to champion it .

sections.label: Section 1299
sections.original: ἐπίκουρος οἰκήτωρ γʼ ἂν οὐκ εἴη χθονός .
sections.translation: No mere ally could enter into an inheritance in my land .

sections.label: Section 1300
sections.original: κἄπειτα τοῦ μέλλειν μʼ ἀπέκτεινες φόβῳ ;
sections.translation: And was it then from fear of consequences that thou didst try to slay me ?

sections.label: Section 1301
sections.original: ὡς μὴ θάνοιμί γʼ , εἰ σὺ μὴ μέλλων τύχοις .
sections.translation: Yes , lest I should myself perish if thou wert spared .

sections.label: Section 1302
sections.original: φθονεῖς ἄπαις οὖσʼ , εἰ πατὴρ ἐξηῦρέ με .
sections.translation: Doth thy childlessness make thee envious that my father found me ?

sections.label: Section 1303
sections.original: σὺ τῶν ἀτέκνων δῆτʼ ἀναρπάσεις δόμους ;
sections.translation: And thou , wilt thou rob the childless of her home ?

sections.label: Section 1304
sections.original: ἡμῖν δέ γʼ ἀλλὰ πατρικῆς οὐκ ἦν μέρος ;
sections.translation: Had I then no share at all in my father ’ s heritage ?

sections.label: Section 1305
sections.original: ὅσʼ ἀσπὶς ἔγχος θʼ · ἥδε σοι παμπησία .
sections.translation: All that his sword and shield had won was thine , and thine alone .

sections.label: Section 1306
sections.original: ἔκλειπε βωμὸν καὶ θεηλάτους ἕδρας .
sections.translation: Quit the altar and sanctuary built for gods .

sections.label: Section 1307
sections.original: τὴν σὴν ὅπου σοι μητέρʼ ἐστὶ νουθέτει .
sections.translation: Go bid thy own mother , wherever she is , do that .

sections.label: Section 1308
sections.original: σὺ δʼ οὐχ ὑφέξεις ζημίαν , κτείνουσʼ ἐμέ ;
sections.translation: Shalt thou escape all punishment , after trying to kill me ?

sections.label: Section 1309
sections.original: ἤν γʼ ἐντὸς ἀδύτων τῶνδέ με σφάξαι θέλῃς .
sections.translation: Not if thou choose to butcher me within this shrine .

sections.label: Section 1310
sections.original: τίς ἡδονή σοι θεοῦ θανεῖν ἐν στέμμασι ;
sections.translation: What joy can it give thee to be slain amid the sacred wreaths ?

sections.label: Section 1311
sections.original: λυπήσομέν τινʼ , ὧν λελυπήμεσθʼ ὕπο .
sections.translation: There is one whom I shall grieve of those who have grieved me .

sections.label: Section 1312
sections.original: φεῦ .
sections.translation: Oh ! ’ tis passing strange how badly the deity hath enacted laws for mortal men , contrary to all sound judgment ; for instance , they should ne ’ er have suffered impious men to sit at their altars ,

sections.label: Section 1315
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐξελαύνειν · οὐδὲ γὰρ ψαύειν καλὸν
sections.translation: but should have driven them away ; for it was nowise right that hands unclean should touch the altars of the gods , though the righteous deserved to find a refuge there from their oppressors , instead of good and bad alike having recourse to the same divine protection with equal success .

sections.label: Section 1320
sections.original: ἐπίσχες , ὦ παῖ · τρίποδα γὰρ χρηστήριον
sections.translation: Refrain thyself , my son ; for I , the priestess of Phoebus , chosen from all the maids of Delphi in accordance with the tripod ’ s ancient rite , have left that prophetic seat , and am passing o ’ er this threshold .

sections.label: Section 1324
sections.original: χαῖρʼ , ὦ φίλη μοι μῆτερ , οὐ τεκοῦσά περ .
sections.translation: Hail to thee , dear mother mine ,— mother , though thou didst not give me birth .

sections.label: Section 1325
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὖν λεγώμεθʼ · ἡ φάτις δʼ οὔ μοι πικρά .
sections.translation: Yes , so have I ever been called , and the title causes me no regret .

sections.label: Section 1326
sections.original: ἤκουσας ὥς μʼ ἔκτεινεν ἥδε μηχαναῖς ;
sections.translation: Hast heard how this woman plotted my death ?

sections.label: Section 1327
sections.original: ἤκουσα · καὶ σὺ δʼ ὠμὸς ὢν ἁμαρτάνεις .
sections.translation: I have ; thou , too , art wrong because of thy harshness .

sections.label: Section 1328
sections.original: οὐ χρή με τοὺς κτείνοντας ἀνταπολλύναι ;
sections.translation: Am I not to pay back murderers in their coin ?

sections.label: Section 1329
sections.original: προγονοῖς δάμαρτες δυσμενεῖς ἀεί ποτε .
sections.translation: Wives ever hate the children of a former marriage .

sections.label: Section 1330
sections.original: ἡμεῖς δὲ μητρυιαῖς γε πάσχοντες κακῶς .
sections.translation: As I hate step-dames for their evil treatment of me .

sections.label: Section 1331
sections.original: μὴ ταῦτα · λείπων ἱερὰ καὶ στείχων πάτραν ⋯
sections.translation: Do not so ; but leaving , as thou art , the shrine , and setting forth for thy country —

sections.label: Section 1332
sections.original: τί δή με δρᾶσαι νουθετούμενον χρεών ;
sections.translation: What then wouldst thou advise me do ?

sections.label: Section 1333
sections.original: καθαρὸς Ἀθήνας ἔλθʼ ὑπʼ οἰωνῶν καλῶν .
sections.translation: With clean hands seek Athens , attended by good omens .

sections.label: Section 1334
sections.original: καθαρὸς ἅπας τοι πολεμίους ὃς ἂν κτάνῃ .
sections.translation: Surely any man hath clean hands who slays his enemies .

sections.label: Section 1335
sections.original: μὴ σύ γε · παρʼ ἡμῶν δʼ ἔκλαβʼ οὓς ἔχω λόγους .
sections.translation: Do not thou do this ; but take the counsel that I have for thee .

sections.label: Section 1336
sections.original: λέγοις ἄν · εὔνους δʼ οὖσʼ ἐρεῖς ὅσʼ ἂν λέγῃς .
sections.translation: Say on ; whate ’ er thou say ’ st will be prompted by thy good will .

sections.label: Section 1337
sections.original: ὁρᾷς τόδʼ ἄγγος χερὸς ὑπʼ ἀγκάλαις ἐμαῖς ;
sections.translation: Dost see this basket that I carry in my arms ?

sections.label: Section 1338
sections.original: ὁρῶ παλαιὰν ἀντίπηγʼ ἐν στέμμασιν .
sections.translation: An ancient ark with chaplets crowned .

sections.label: Section 1339
sections.original: ἐν τῇδέ σʼ ἔλαβον νεόγονον βρέφος ποτέ .
sections.translation: Herein I found thee long ago , a newborn babe .

sections.label: Section 1340
sections.original: τί φῄς ; ὁ μῦθος εἰσενήνεκται νέος .
sections.translation: What sayest thou ? there is novelty in the story thou art introducing .

sections.label: Section 1341
sections.original: σιγῇ γὰρ εἶχον αὐτά · νῦν δὲ δείκνυμεν .
sections.translation: Yea , for I was keeping these relics a secret , but now I show them .

sections.label: Section 1342
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ἔκρυπτες τόδε λαβοῦσʼ ἡμᾶς πάλαι ;
sections.translation: How earnest thou to hide them on that day , now long ago , when thou didst find me ?

sections.label: Section 1343
sections.original: ὁ θεός σʼ ἐβούλετʼ ἐν δόμοις ἔχειν λάτριν .
sections.translation: The god wished to have thee as his servant in his courts .

sections.label: Section 1344
sections.original: νῦν δʼ οὐχὶ χρῄζει ; τῷ τόδε γνῶναί με χρή ;
sections.translation: Does he no longer wish it ? How am I to know this ?

sections.label: Section 1345
sections.original: πατέρα κατειπὼν τῆσδέ σʼ ἐκπέμπει χθονός .
sections.translation: By declaring to thee thy sire , he dismisses thee from this land .

sections.label: Section 1346
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἐκ κελευσμῶν ἢ πόθεν σῴζεις τάδε ;
sections.translation: Is it by his command thou keepest these relics , or why ?
